EXHIBIT A TO REPLY TO OPPOSITION T DEMURRER FILED BY DEFENDANT SYMPHONY COMMERCE, INC.
MINI MINUTES FOR JUL-25-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SYMPHONY COMMERCE, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO AUGUST 22, 2014 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 TO GIVE OPPOSING PARTY THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. MOVING PARTY MUST DELIVER A COURTESY COPY OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN JULY 25, 2014 AT 4:00 PM. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING AND REPLY PAPERS. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR# 5976.
MINI MINUTES FOR AUG-22-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
ORDER OVERRULING SYMPHONY COMMERCE, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SYMPHONY COMMERCE, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT - THE COURT OVERRULES THE DEMURRER IN ITS ENTIRETY. THE CONTRACT IS NOT AN "AGREEMENT TO AGREE." PARAGRAPHS 6-11 OF THE COMPLAINT CLEARLY ALLEGE A BREACH OF CONTRACT CAUSE OF ACTION AND PLAINTIFF EVEN ATTACHES A COPY OF THE PARTIES' WRITTEN AGREEMENT. DEFENDANT DOES NOT SHOW THAT PLAINTIFF'S CONSTRUCTION OF THE CONTRACT IS ERRONEOUS. A DEMURRER TO A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT ADMITS NOT ONLY THE CONTENTS OF THE CONTRACT, BUT ALSO ANY PLEADED MEANING THAT THE INSTRUMENT IS REASONABLY SUSCEPTIBLE. SEE ARAGON-HAAS V. FAMILY SECURITY INS. SERVICES, INC., (1991) 231 CAL.APP.3D 232, 239 ("WHILE PLAINTIFFS' INTERPRETATION OF THE CONTRACT MAY PROVE INVALID, IT [IS] IMPROPER TO RESOLVE THE ISSUE AGAINST HER SOLELY ON HER OWN PLEADING. 'IN RULING ON A DEMURRER, THE LIKELIHOOD THAT THE PLEADING WILL BE ABLE TO PROVE HIS ALLEGATIONS IS NOT THE QUESTION.'"). DEFENDANT ARGUES THAT PLAINTIFF NEEDS TO PLEAD MORE FACTS REGARDING HOW PLAINTIFF PERFORMED THE CONTRACT, E.G., VETTING, INTERVIEWING. CCP 457 EXPRESSLY PROVIDES THAT A PARTY ALLEGING A BREACH OF CONTRACT CAN PLEAD ITS PERFORMANCE GENERALLY. THE COMMON COUNTS CAUSE OF ACTION IS WELL PLEADED AND A FACTUAL ISSUE REMAINS WHETHER PLAINTIFF PERFORMED THE REQUIRED SERVICES. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST J. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976 (302/EHG)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-01-2014 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-15-2014 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Dec-16-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-03-2014 Continued To Oct-29-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-22-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT SYMPHONY COMMERCE, INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT - The court overrules the demurrer in its entirety. The contract is not an "agreement to agree." Paragraphs 6-11 of the complaint clearly allege a breach of contract cause of action and plaintiff even attaches a copy of the parties' written agreement. Defendant does not show that plaintiff's construction of the contract is erroneous. A demurrer to a cause of action for breach of contract admits not only the contents of the contract, but also any pleaded meaning that the instrument is reasonably susceptible. See Aragon-Haas v. Family Security Ins. Services, Inc., (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 232, 239 ("While plaintiffs' interpretation of the contract may prove invalid, it [is] improper to resolve the issue against her solely on her own pleading. 'In ruling on a demurrer, the likelihood that the pleading will be able to prove his allegations is not the question.'"). Defendant argues that plaintiff needs to plead more facts regarding how plaintiff performed the contract, e.g., vetting, interviewing. CCP 457 expressly provides that a party alleging a breach of contract can plead its performance generally. The common counts cause of action is well pleaded and a factual issue remains whether plaintiff performed the required services. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest J. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/eg

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-25-2014 Continued To Aug-22-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The tentative ruling is adopted. DEFENDANT SYMPHONY COMMERCE, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is continued to August 22, 2014 at 9:30 am in Dept. 302 to give opposing party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must deliver a courtesy copy of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than July 25, 2014 at 4:00 pm. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The Court has courtesy copies of the moving and reply papers. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.
